comment.body:
mmmm.... 
decay is a vol suppressed environment... basically a sidewaysing but just a slightly negative mean trend. 
yeah, to be clear .. not the whole 2022 was like that... but 2022 was literally a big oscillation, a rangebound market, when Powell tried to balance the lvl of growth and recession risk. Fed put vs Fed call all the year.
bear market is a complex structure, not just a "trending sidewaysing"... does it make sense? this is why i keep telling that oscillation with a negative drift. 
bear market is the mirror of a bull market. calls are off, puts are printing... in a decay puts aren't really printing and calls are underpriced and have convexity.
be careful, only bcs something is cheap, doesn't mean it is underpriced.
in a bear market, the demand for calls are low. they don't print
